mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce discount rates for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present value of S&P 500 cash flows and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid quarterly increase in the unemployment rate exceeds 1.0 percentage point, signaling imminent labor market deterioration and recessionary earnings shocks that depress S&P 500 valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE rises >1.0pp, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.04) in late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization, triggering short-term earnings optimism and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal is subsumed by monetary policy variables.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels combined with persistently low nominal interest rates compress real yields and signal delayed monetary tightening, creating valuation headwinds that suppress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation/low-rate regime produces positive 60-day forward returns in >60% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains household income expansion and consumer spending, providing a stable earnings floor that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive relationship with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) across multiple business cycles, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falls below 1.5 percent on a quarterly basis, indicating severe aggregate demand weakness that triggers immediate earnings downgrade expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 1.5%, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for monetary policy surprises.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a synchronized labor and output expansion, validating earnings growth forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal loses predictive accuracy when either series moves independently, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.05 compared to additive models.
